issue_title: No "show channel details" in long-press menu in feed or local playlist items
issue_body:
<!--
Oh no, a bug! It happens. Thanks for reporting an issue with NewPipe. To make it easier for us to help you please enter detailed information in the template we have provided below. If a section isn't relevant, just delete it, though it would be helpful to still provide as much detail as possible.
-->

<!-- IF YOU DON'T FILL IN THE TEMPLATE PROPERLY, YOUR ISSUE IS LIABLE TO BE CLOSED. If you feel tired/lazy right now, open your issue some other time. We'll wait. -->

<!-- The comments between these brackets won't show up in the submitted issue (as you can see in the Preview). -->

### Checklist
<!-- This checklist is COMPULSORY. The first box has been checked for you to show you how it is done. -->

- [x] I am using the latest version - 0.21.2 RC/latest dev build <!-- Check https://github.com/TeamNewPipe/NewPipe/releases -->
- [x] I checked, but didn't find any duplicates (open OR closed) of this issue in the repo. <!-- Seriously, check. O_O -->
- [x] I have read the contribution guidelines given at https://github.com/TeamNewPipe/NewPipe/blob/HEAD/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md.
- [x] This issue contains only one bug. I will open one issue for every bug report I want to file.

### Steps to reproduce the bug
1. Open any feed (e.g. What's New) or local playlist
2. Long press on an item

<!-- If you can't cause the bug to show up again reliably (and hence don't have a proper set of steps to give us), please still try to give as many details as possible on how you think you encountered the bug. -->

### Actual behaviour
<!-- Tell us what happens with the steps given above. -->
There's no "show channel details" shown in the menu.


### Expected behavior
<!-- Tell us what you expect to happen. -->
There should be a "show channel details" option in the meu.

### Additional context
https://github.com/TeamNewPipe/NewPipe/pull/5851#issuecomment-823188251

### Device info
This is platform-independent, but I filled this out for completeness.

 - Android version/Custom ROM version: Android 11
 - Device model: Galaxy A51

@opusforlife2
pr_title: Add Show Channel Details where it's missing
pr_body:
#### What is it? - [x] Bugfix (user facing) - [x] Feature (user facing) - [ ] Codebase improvement (dev facing) - [ ] Meta improvement to the project (dev facing) #### Description of the changes in your PR - Add `Show Channel Details` to `Local Playlists`, `Watch History` and `Subscription Feeds` - Had to update [StreamEntity](https://github.com/TeamNewPipe/NewPipe/blob/dev/app/src/main/java/org/schabi/newpipe/database/stream/model/StreamEntity.kt) to add a `uploader_url` column. Also added a database `Migration` and changed the version number to `4`. - If you click `Show Channel Details` and the `uploader_url` is empty in the database, it fetches it using the `Newpipe Extractor` and saves it to the database. - Had to add `kapt "org.xerial:sqlite-jdbc:3.34.0"` to `build.gradle` as a [workaround](https://issuetracker.google.com/issues/174695268#comment9) on M1 Macs. Doesn't seem to hurt the build on other machines so I left it in. #### Before/After Screenshots/Screen Record - Before: - After: #### Fixes the following issue(s) - Fixes #6155 #### TODO: - Show some kind of loading indicator while loading the `uploader_url` from the `Newpipe Extractor`. - Fix [AppDatabaseTest](https://github.com/TeamNewPipe/NewPipe/blob/dev/app/src/androidTest/java/org/schabi/newpipe/database/AppDatabaseTest.kt) ([My SO question](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/68757574/android-room-partial-migration-testing))
Options Option 1: Just test migrating directly from `2` to `4`.
Option 2: Use SupportSQLiteDatabase query and DatabaseUtils.dumpCusrorToString.
Option 3: Hopefully someone knows the correct way to do it...
#### APK testing The APK can be found by going to the "Checks" tab below the title. On the left pane, click on "CI", scroll down to "artifacts" and click "app" to download the zip file which contains the debug APK of this PR. #### Due diligence - [x] I read the [contribution guidelines](https://github.com/TeamNewPipe/NewPipe/blob/HEAD/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md).
